events.reflexive_note: Reading around the archive widened the project scope and showed that classification itself might be a design act rather than just a storage problem.
Reading around the archive widened the project scope and showed that classification itself might be a design act rather than just a storage problem.
Talking through colleagues's methods made me realise I wanted a process that could hold digital artefacts, metadata notes and computational experiments together.
Talking through colleagues's methods made me realise I wanted a process that could hold digital artefacts, metadata notes and computational experiments together.
The discussion forced me to define whether the PhD was about building a system or using systems as a way to think.
The conversation exposed that my research area was still too broad which helped narrow the next ideation.
Writing explanatory boilerplate showed that interface language is part of the argument not just a layer of support text.
The glossary slowed the pace in a useful way by forcing me to name terms precisely before building around them.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

events.reflexive_note: Documenting the taxonomy made the hidden logic of the system visible enough to question and revise.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

events.reflexive_note: Clear editorial definitions helped me justify Sankey as showing transformation rather than mere decoration.
events.source_label: FT charting and data storytelling references

events.reflexive_note: Writing the research instrument statement clarified that the apps were instruments of inquiry rather than outputs alone.
Peer questioned overlap between theme and tags which prompted cleaner category boundaries.
Writing the lead paragraph clarified that the Sankey is provisional and co-evolving with the data.
events.source_label: Intro paragraph and framing copy pass

events.reflexive_note: The meeting reinforced the need to show uncertainty and gaps rather than smoothing them away for elegance.
events.source_label: Supervisor meeting on ethics and representation

events.reflexive_note: The hand-drawn map showed that graphic design, archives  and machine learning were already entangled in my thinking even before I had language for it.
The hand-drawn map showed that graphic design, archives  and machine learning were already entangled in my thinking even before I had language for it.
The comparison highlighted inconsistent descriptions reinforcing that data cleaning is also testamentary trace cleaning.
The synthesis board showed how often I described decisions as inevitable; I started rewriting them as choices.
Drawing the literature as a situated diagram helped align the build with the argument rather than treat it as an add-on.
The ecosystem diagram made it obvious that public archive admin layer and ML environment were one research system.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

events.reflexive_note: Anomalies and missing records became productive rather than annoying once I treated them as traces of institutional process.
Pulling the APR materials together made it possible to name the three apps as a single research environment.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
events.source_label: Public archive API build, admin layer and ML environment articulated as one practice

events.reflexive_note: Linking the supporting repositories to the main platforms showed that process documentation is part of the research apparatus.
events.source_label: Coded research journal and supporting repositories aligned with main apps

events.reflexive_note: Juxtaposing institutional metadata with my own labels revealed where my values and theirs diverged.
events.source_label: Comparison of official vs personal labels

events.reflexive_note: Re-reading the whole journal made recurring anxieties visible: over-framing over-designing and then learning to slow down."
events.source_label: Review of year-one notes and annotations

events.reflexive_note: The synthesis map suggested year two could compare institutional structures with my own emerging taxonomies more explicitly.
I started to see visual analytics not as a neutral reporting tool but as something that could surface my own decision-making in the research.
I started to see visual analytics not as a neutral reporting tool but as something that could surface my own decision-making in the research.
Realised I was framing the work too much around the API and not enough around critical insights and analysis.
The archivist's language around provenance sharpened how I thought about tracing influence without pretending causality.
events.source_label: Discussion with Brighton archivist on categorisation

events.reflexive_note: Supervision clarified that the value of the build lay not in technical novelty but in what it made thinkable and discussable.
The supervision helped me see the apps as operationalising testamentary tracing rather than merely illustrating it.
I started to treat omission, emphasis and smoothing as ethical acts in the interface not merely aesthetic ones.
The seminar gave language for treating my coded dataset as situated rather than incomplete.
events.source_label: Research methods seminar on situated knowledge

events.reflexive_note: What had felt like a blockage became analytically useful once I treated redaction as evidence of institutional control.
events.source_label: Redacted boxes treated as design constraint

events.reflexive_note: I started to think of the archive itself as an interaction design problem not just a source of content.
Freezing further feature work was difficult but productive: it revealed how easily building can displace analysis.
events.source_label: Freeze practice development and turn material into argument

events.reflexive_note: The reading helped justify a dataset made of coded practice events rather than only conventional metrics.

links.source: coding:Database authorities cleaning test in PostgreSQL

events.reflexive_note: Cleaning a small archival dataset exposed how much interpretation hides inside supposedly basic preprocessing.
events.source_label: Database authorities cleaning test in PostgreSQL

events.reflexive_note: Cleaning a small archival dataset exposed how much interpretation hides inside supposedly basic preprocessing.
events.source_label: Database authorities cleaning test in PostgreSQL

events.reflexive_note: The visit fed directly back into schema revision: the records resisted neat categorisation in practice.
events.source_label: Taxonomy schema iteration after archive visit

events.reflexive_note: Testing ABBEY / OCR workflows made handwriting and scan quality into methodological variables rather than minor technical issues.
Reducing decorative treatment made the work read more as a research instrument than a portfolio piece.
events.source_label: Dark mode and dashboard restraint test

events.reflexive_note: A second visit sharpened what the system would have to preserve about provenance sequence and material context.
Weighting by intensity changed the story dramatically reminding me that value design is itself an argument.
events.source_label: Value weighting test using intensity score

events.reflexive_note: Using too many colours made the categories feel fixed; a more restrained palette kept room for ambiguity.
The discussion forced me to define whether the PhD was about building a system or using systems as a way to think.

links.source: archival research:Visit to RCA special collections

events.reflexive_note: The metadata structure felt authoritative and brittle at once; I noted how visual order can hide archival bias.
The metadata structure felt authoritative and brittle at once; I noted how visual order can hide archival bias.
I was drawn to reduction techniques visually but noted the risk of mistaking a compelling map for an explanation.
Drafting a proposal to secure more autonomy made clear that access is not just logistical but methodological.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
events.source_label: V&A research project proposal for greater autonomy

events.reflexive_note: The access discussion confirmed that institutional restriction is part of the research condition not just an obstacle around it.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
events.source_label: V&A conference call on access constraints

events.reflexive_note: I began separating contribution to method from contribution to artefact in my notes.
Realised I was framing the work too much around the API and not enough around critical insights and analysis.

events.reflexive_note: Drafting the statement of work made the archive feel less like a vague aspiration and more like a research instrument.

events.reflexive_note: Transforming journal entries into source-action-outcome rows made the research process feel materially computable.
Typographic hierarchy started to separate evidence, interpretation and reflection more clearly.
events.source_label: Typography pass on coded journal prototype

events.reflexive_note: Toning down labels gave more space to the flows and notes which improved readability without losing nuance.
Bringing transcriptions into the environment linked archival record and spoken recollection in one research space.
events.source_label: Oral-history transcription workflow into research environment

events.reflexive_note: Working through master and derivative assets made infrastructure visible as part of the argument rather than background support.
events.source_label: Cloud storage and asset pipeline stabilisation

links.source: supervision:Practice positioned as MVP in supervision

events.reflexive_note: Framing the practice as an MVP lowered the pressure to make a finished product and made iteration feel legitimate.
events.source_label: Practice positioned as MVP in supervision

events.reflexive_note: Framing the practice as an MVP lowered the pressure to make a finished product and made iteration feel legitimate.
events.source_label: Practice positioned as MVP in supervision

events.reflexive_note: I began distinguishing reflection on outcomes from reflexivity about how I shaped those outcomes.
The supervision pushed me to explain the apps in terms a contemporary research audience could understand.
events.source_label: Rewrite build narrative for contemporary research audience

events.reflexive_note: Feedback suggested the copy still sounded too certain; I rewrote labels to acknowledge interpretation and choice.
Writing up the lightweight architecture made constraint look methodologically productive rather than merely limiting.
events.source_label: Lean CPU-only ML architecture clarified as methodological choice

events.reflexive_note: Rehearsing the narrative revealed where I was still making neat causal claims from messy sequences.
Designing the timeline made the project legible to me; the act of layout became a form of analysis.
Designing the timeline made the project legible to me; the act of layout became a form of analysis.
Monthly grouping gave the project a narrative spine without forcing chronology into every flow.

links.source: coding:Keyword extraction test on archival records

events.reflexive_note: I treated the records as stable text objects and got shallow results; the failure exposed how messy the source material really was.
events.source_label: Keyword extraction test on archival records

events.reflexive_note: I treated the records as stable text objects and got shallow results; the failure exposed how messy the source material really was.
events.source_label: Keyword extraction test on archival records

events.reflexive_note: The topics were too generic to be useful; the failure reminded me that my corpus is small and heavily curated by me.
events.source_label: Topic modelling test on reading corpus

events.reflexive_note: The retrieval tests showed that computational patterning can look persuasive before it becomes meaningful.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

events.reflexive_note: Auto-summaries erased nuance and uncertainty which undermined the reflexive quality I wanted to preserve.
events.source_label: Small language model test for note summarisation

events.reflexive_note: The conversation exposed that my research area was still too broad which helped narrow the next ideation.

events.reflexive_note: Seeing everything on the wall revealed clusters of concern that were less obvious on screen especially around classification and narrative.

events.reflexive_note: I became more aware that descriptive language in archives carries institutional voice which I may also reproduce in my own labels.

events.reflexive_note: Returning after the review  I noticed how much my own category choices were mirroring institutional taxonomies.
events.source_label: Revisit to digital catalogue with new lens

events.reflexive_note: Handling physical records reminded me that the digital visualisation should still register material texture and absence.
With the interface further along I could see the archives less as raw material and more as collaborators in framing the project.
events.source_label: Return to source material after prototype maturity

events.reflexive_note: Getting the GraphQL gateway working turned scattered archival ambitions into an operational research environment.

links.source: data science:Cluster analysis on tagged references

events.reflexive_note: The clusters were weak but the process suggested that my hand-made categories were more revealing than automated groupings at this stage.
The clusters were weak but the process suggested that my hand-made categories were more revealing than automated groupings at this stage.
Peer feedback pushed me to test process documentation itself as material  not just support evidence.
The graph was more evocative than accurate but it suggested a parallel way of showing archival resemblance beyond metadata.
Different weighting strategies produced different stories making the interface feel more like an argument than an answer.
events.source_label: Node weighting by confidence vs intensity

events.reflexive_note: Supervision clarified that the value of the build lay not in technical novelty but in what it made thinkable and discussable.

events.source_label: Research methods seminar on situated knowledge

events.reflexive_note: Anomalies and missing records became productive rather than annoying once I treated them as traces of institutional process.
I began separating contribution to method from contribution to artefact in my notes.

links.source: archival research:Redacted boxes treated as design constraint

events.reflexive_note: What had felt like a blockage became analytically useful once I treated redaction as evidence of institutional control.
events.source_label: Redacted boxes treated as design constraint

events.reflexive_note: Making the chart adapt to screen width exposed what was structurally essential in the visualisation.
I started to think of the archive itself as an interaction design problem not just a source of content.

links.source: graphic design:Colour system test for themes

events.reflexive_note: Using too many colours made the categories feel fixed; a more restrained palette kept room for ambiguity.

links.source: coding:Oral-history transcription workflow into research environment

events.reflexive_note: Bringing transcriptions into the environment linked archival record and spoken recollection in one research space.
events.source_label: Oral-history transcription workflow into research environment

events.reflexive_note: Monthly grouping gave the project a narrative spine without forcing chronology into every flow.

links.source: machine learning:Similarity graph from image embeddings

events.reflexive_note: The graph was more evocative than accurate but it suggested a parallel way of showing archival resemblance beyond metadata.

links.source: supervision:Freeze practice development and turn material into argument

events.reflexive_note: Freezing further feature work was difficult but productive: it revealed how easily building can displace analysis.
events.source_label: Freeze practice development and turn material into argument

events.reflexive_note: Feedback suggested the copy still sounded too certain; I rewrote labels to acknowledge interpretation and choice.

links.source: archival research:Diagramming ML data science space

events.reflexive_note: Handling physical records reminded me that the digital visualisation should still register material texture and absence.

links.source: graphic design:API demo video for APR

events.reflexive_note: Recording the demo turned the build into a communicable research object and exposed where my explanation still lagged behind the system.
Search made the dataset navigable as a journal not just a chart.

links.source: conversation:Coffee chat with data scientist friend

events.reflexive_note: The chat challenged my use of 'model' and 'dataset' which pushed me to be more precise without becoming reductive.
events.source_label: Coffee chat with data scientist friend

links.source: prototyping:Public archive API build, admin layer and ML environment articulated as one practice

events.reflexive_note: Pulling the APR materials together made it possible to name the three apps as a single research environment.
events.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
events.source_label: Public archive API build, admin layer and ML environment articulated as one practice

links.source: coding:Dummy dataset generation for stress test

events.reflexive_note: Scaling the dataset exposed where the visual logic held and where it collapsed into noise.
events.source_label: Dummy dataset generation for stress test

links.source: coding:Cloud storage and asset pipeline stabilisation

events.reflexive_note: Working through master and derivative assets made infrastructure visible as part of the argument rather than background support.
events.source_label: Cloud storage and asset pipeline stabilisation

links.source: graphic design:Intro paragraph and framing copy pass

events.reflexive_note: Writing the lead paragraph clarified that the Sankey is provisional and co-evolving with the data.
events.source_label: Intro paragraph and framing copy pass

links.source: graphic design:Coded research journal and supporting repositories aligned with main apps

events.reflexive_note: Linking the supporting repositories to the main platforms showed that process documentation is part of the research apparatus.
events.source_label: Coded research journal and supporting repositories aligned with main apps

events.reflexive_note: Comparing Sankey, chord  and timeline patterns confirmed that process flow best matched the research question.
Comparing Sankey, chord  and timeline patterns confirmed that process flow best matched the research question.
The reading helped justify a dataset made of coded practice events rather than only conventional metrics.

links.source: machine learning:Active learning idea for assisted tagging

events.reflexive_note: Assisted tagging looked promising but I worried about outsourcing interpretive labour too early.
events.source_label: Active learning idea for assisted tagging

links.source: machine learning:Lean CPU-only ML architecture clarified as methodological choice

events.reflexive_note: Writing up the lightweight architecture made constraint look methodologically productive rather than merely limiting.
events.source_label: Lean CPU-only ML architecture clarified as methodological choice

events.reflexive_note: Juxtaposing institutional metadata with my own labels revealed where my values and theirs diverged.
events.source_label: Comparison of official vs personal labels

events.reflexive_note: Different weighting strategies produced different stories making the interface feel more like an argument than an answer.
events.source_label: Node weighting by confidence vs intensity

events.reflexive_note: The meeting reinforced the need to show uncertainty and gaps rather than smoothing them away for elegance.
events.source_label: Supervisor meeting on ethics and representation

events.reflexive_note: With the interface further along I could see the archives less as raw material and more as collaborators in framing the project.
events.source_label: Return to source material after prototype maturity

events.reflexive_note: Rehearsing the narrative revealed where I was still making neat causal claims from messy sequences.

links.source: coding:Machine learning MVP stress test working

events.reflexive_note: The pipeline made the project operational: data entry cleaning and visualisation finally felt like one research loop.
events.source_label: Machine learning MVP stress test working

events.reflexive_note: The synthesis map suggested year two could compare institutional structures with my own emerging taxonomies more explicitly.

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Reading around the archive widened the project scope and showed that classification itself might be a design act rather than just a storage problem.
Talking through colleagues's methods made me realise I wanted a process that could hold digital artefacts, metadata notes and computational experiments together.
The hand-drawn map showed that graphic design, archives  and machine learning were already entangled in my thinking even before I had language for it.
I started to see visual analytics not as a neutral reporting tool but as something that could surface my own decision-making in the research.
Cleaning a small archival dataset exposed how much interpretation hides inside supposedly basic preprocessing.
rawEvents.source_label: Database authorities cleaning test in PostgreSQL

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The discussion forced me to define whether the PhD was about building a system or using systems as a way to think.
The metadata structure felt authoritative and brittle at once; I noted how visual order can hide archival bias.
Realised I was framing the work too much around the API and not enough around critical insights and analysis.
Drafting the statement of work made the archive feel less like a vague aspiration and more like a research instrument.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Review of practice 9.docx; Kanban wip board; SR1 09 Oct 2025
rawEvents.source_label: API statement of work + first archive system sketch

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Framing the practice as an MVP lowered the pressure to make a finished product and made iteration feel legitimate.
rawEvents.source_label: Practice positioned as MVP in supervision

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Designing the timeline made the project legible to me; the act of layout became a form of analysis.
I treated the records as stable text objects and got shallow results; the failure exposed how messy the source material really was.
rawEvents.source_label: Keyword extraction test on archival records

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The conversation exposed that my research area was still too broad which helped narrow the next ideation.
Seeing everything on the wall revealed clusters of concern that were less obvious on screen especially around classification and narrative.
rawEvents.source_label: Miro board of printed references and outputs

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Getting the GraphQL gateway working turned scattered archival ambitions into an operational research environment.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Writing explanatory boilerplate showed that interface language is part of the argument not just a layer of support text.
I was drawn to reduction techniques visually but noted the risk of mistaking a compelling map for an explanation.
Building the ingestion schema exposed how much interpretation sits inside categorisation itself.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The archivist's language around provenance sharpened how I thought about tracing influence without pretending causality.
rawEvents.source_label: Discussion with Brighton archivist on categorisation

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The discussion suggested the authorities itself could become the dataset rather than merely supporting one.
I was overcommitting to form before I had a clear rationale for why this interaction mattered.
The glossary slowed the pace in a useful way by forcing me to name terms precisely before building around them.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Transforming journal entries into source-action-outcome rows made the research process feel materially computable.
I became more aware that descriptive language in archives carries institutional voice which I may also reproduce in my own labels.
rawEvents.source_label: Scan of finding aids and catalogue language

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The grid imposed order on messy material without flattening it too much; that balance felt important.
rawEvents.source_label: Grid system experiment for journal UI

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Seeing the material in situ shifted the build from abstract infrastructure to a response to real records.
The visit fed directly back into schema revision: the records resisted neat categorisation in practice.
rawEvents.source_label: Taxonomy schema iteration after archive visit

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The paper made me ask whether I was critiquing machine learning from outside it or trying to work from within its constraints.
Data engineering felt less like cleaning and more like interpretation under pressure.
rawEvents.source_label: Data engineering pass on archive records

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The clusters were weak but the process suggested that my hand-made categories were more revealing than automated groupings at this stage.
Supervision clarified that the value of the build lay not in technical novelty but in what it made thinkable and discussable.
Peer feedback pushed me to test process documentation itself as material  not just support evidence.
Adding my own notes to hover states made the visualisation feel authored rather than dashboard-like.
Once the database and query layer started working together the project finally felt like a coherent system rather than a set of parts.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Drafting a proposal to secure more autonomy made clear that access is not just logistical but methodological.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
rawEvents.source_label: V&A research project proposal for greater autonomy

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Documenting the taxonomy made the hidden logic of the system visible enough to question and revise.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Clear editorial definitions helped me justify Sankey as showing transformation rather than mere decoration.
rawEvents.source_label: FT charting and data storytelling references

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Building a rough visualisation made the research feel more situated in my own practice rather than borrowed methods.
The model surfaced visual similarity  but I wasn't yet sure what a meaningful similarity looked like in this context.
rawEvents.source_label: Image embedding test on archival scans

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Early measurement experiments were useful less for their outputs than for showing what the data could not yet support.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
rawEvents.source_label: DDR archive API paired with testamentary traces measurement tests

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Typographic hierarchy started to separate evidence, interpretation and reflection more clearly.
rawEvents.source_label: Typography pass on coded journal prototype

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The comparison highlighted inconsistent descriptions reinforcing that data cleaning is also testamentary trace cleaning.
The supervision helped me see the apps as operationalising testamentary tracing rather than merely illustrating it.
Seeing the custom component architecture made the project feel technically viable rather than speculative.
rawEvents.source_label: Code clinic on D3 and React integration

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The synthesis board showed how often I described decisions as inevitable; I started rewriting them as choices.
A working MVP changed the tempo of the research: I could test archival ideas through use rather than only description.
rawEvents.source_label: API demonstration and MVP ingestion workflow

rawEvents.reflexive_note: I began distinguishing reflection on outcomes from reflexivity about how I shaped those outcomes.
Testing ABBEY / OCR workflows made handwriting and scan quality into methodological variables rather than minor technical issues.
The dataset finally felt stable enough to circulate between Excel, Python and the prototype app.
rawEvents.source_label: Cleaned export of coded journal into CSV

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Drawing the literature as a situated diagram helped align the build with the argument rather than treat it as an add-on.
The review made me ask whether I was merely documenting process or actually exposing how my own choices shaped it.
Writing the research instrument statement clarified that the apps were instruments of inquiry rather than outputs alone.
Reducing decorative treatment made the work read more as a research instrument than a portfolio piece.
rawEvents.source_label: Dark mode and dashboard restraint test

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The topics were too generic to be useful; the failure reminded me that my corpus is small and heavily curated by me.
rawEvents.source_label: Topic modelling test on reading corpus

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The oral history training widened the build beyond records and metadata suggesting a more relational research environment.
rawEvents.source_label: Oral history course folded into app planning

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Peer questioned overlap between theme and tags which prompted cleaner category boundaries.
The ecosystem diagram made it obvious that public archive admin layer and ML environment were one research system.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The click-to-pin interaction gave the Sankey enough depth to carry reflective writing without clutter.
A second visit sharpened what the system would have to preserve about provenance sequence and material context.
Returning after the review  I noticed how much my own category choices were mirroring institutional taxonomies.
rawEvents.source_label: Revisit to digital catalogue with new lens

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Ingestion made the build confront real heterogeneity in the material  forcing the system to become more flexible.
rawEvents.source_label: Ingest V&A East Storehouse DDR material

rawEvents.reflexive_note: I started to treat omission, emphasis and smoothing as ethical acts in the interface not merely aesthetic ones.
Filters let the same data tell different stories which felt closer to reflexive reading than a single master narrative.
rawEvents.source_label: GraphQl parsing to Granite ML model

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Weighting by intensity changed the story dramatically reminding me that value design is itself an argument.
rawEvents.source_label: Value weighting test using intensity score

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The retrieval tests showed that computational patterning can look persuasive before it becomes meaningful.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx

rawEvents.reflexive_note: The supervision pushed me to explain the apps in terms a contemporary research audience could understand.
rawEvents.source_label: Rewrite build narrative for contemporary research audience

rawEvents.reflexive_note: Toning down labels gave more space to the flows and notes which improved readability without losing nuance.
The idea of semi-automating tags was tempting but I worried it would erase the reflexive labour I was trying to preserve.
The access discussion confirmed that institutional restriction is part of the research condition not just an obstacle around it.
rawEvents.evidence_link: Kanban wip board; Review of practice 9.docx
rawEvents.source_label: V&A conference call on access constraints
